rxn_class_summary: Electrochemical three-component thio-perfluoroalkylation of alkenes with thiophenols and perfluoroalkyl iodides under FeCl2 catalysis to give vicinal thioether/perfluoroalkyl products.
electrochemical_role: Anodic oxidation of an Fe(II)-thiolate complex (C) to an Fe(III) species (D), with concomitant cathodic reduction of D back to release product and regenerate Fe(II); perfluoroalkyl radical is generated via halogen-bonding activation of nC4F9I by TMEDA/H2O rather than direct electrolysis.

key_intermediates.name: Halogen-bond (XB) complex of nC4F9I with TMEDA/H2O
Benzylic carbon radical B from nC4F9 addition to styrene
Fe(III)-thiolate species D after anodic oxidation
Perfluoroalkyl iodide adduct C (alkyl iodide on styrene)

electron_flow: TMEDA/H2O activates nC4F9I via halogen bonding to release nC4F9 radical, which adds to the alkene giving benzylic radical B; in parallel, ArSH coordinates Fe(II) (C), is oxidized at the anode to Fe(III)-thiolate D, which couples with radical B and is reduced at the cathode to deliver the product while regenerating Fe(II).

probe_reagents.name: TEMPO
probe_reagents.observation: Product 4aa only in trace amounts; TEMPO–nC4F9-alkyl adduct B detected by LCMS, supporting perfluoroalkyl/benzyl radical intermediates.

probe_reagents.name: Diaryl disulfide 2a' (4-MeO-C6H4-S-S-C6H4-4-OMe)
probe_reagents.observation: Using disulfide instead of thiol gives only trace 4aa, indicating disulfide is not the productive intermediate.

probe_reagents.name: nC4F9I + alkene without thiol (control for iodide adduct C)
probe_reagents.observation: Iodo-perfluoroalkylated alkene C not detected (n.d.), ruling out ATRA-type alkyl iodide as the intermediate.

probe_reagents.name: Cyclic voltammetry (with/without FeCl2)
probe_reagents.observation: Eox decreases from 3.43 V to 3.23 V upon addition of FeCl2, indicating FeCl2 lowers the oxidation potential and mediates anodic oxidation.

notes: Authors explicitly exclude two alternative pathways: (i) disulfide-mediated thiyl radical pathway and (ii) ATRA-type iodoperfluoroalkylation followed by SN2 with thiolate, since both surrogate intermediates fail to give product under standard conditions. The perfluoroalkyl radical is proposed to form via halogen-bonding activation rather than direct cathodic reduction of C-I.
